tip_save: Downloads your data as a JSON file. Use it to restore your card on any device.
tip_load: Load a previously saved JSON file to restore all your data.
tip_clear: Clears all fields. This cannot be undone.
tip_saveload: Save your data as a JSON file or load a previously saved file.
tip_download_pdf: Choose languages and pay €2, or use a code for a free download.
tip_printclear: Download the card as PDF or clear all fields.
pdf_no_lang: Please select at least one language.
pdf_error: Something went wrong. Please try again.
pdf_error_402: Payment not confirmed yet. Please try again in a moment.
pdf_error_403: Code or session invalid or already used.
pdf_error_503: PDF service temporarily unavailable. You will receive an email with a retry link.
tip_f_type: Type of vessel, e.g. motor yacht, sailboat, inland barge.
tip_f_eni: 8-digit European Vessel Identification Number (inland waterways only).
tip_f_draft: Draught at normal load in metres.
tip_f_airdraft: Height above water in metres (relevant for bridges).
tip_f_alt_length: Total length including lowered mast in metres.
tip_f_alt_airdraft: Height above water with raised mast in metres.
tip_mmsi: 9-digit MMSI number for DSC and AIS.
clear_confirm: Clear all data? This cannot be undone.
info_what_body: MareSafe provides a template for a personal emergency card in A4 format. Fill in your vessel details and print — the card contains all information you need in an emergency on the water. This card is configured for the Netherlands.
info_save_body: Your data is automatically saved in this browser. Use Save data to download a backup and load it on another device via Load data.
info_print_body: Click Print / PDF at the bottom of the form. Choose Destination → Save as PDF for a digital copy, or print directly. Make sure Print backgrounds is enabled for colours.
info_print_mobile: Printing and creating a PDF from mobile is not fully supported yet. On a Mac or PC it works perfectly.
warn_body: The browser may lose saved data after an update or when clearing browser data. We always recommend saving your data manually via Save data at the bottom of the form.
vw_body: You have loaded an older saved version. All available data has been loaded. Save your data to update to the current version.
card_subtitle: Keep this document accessible while sailing
calls_english_note: Emergency calls are made internationally in English.
mayday_when: Life in danger: sinking / fire / man overboard
call_note: Note: only the skipper may order a MAYDAY or PAN PAN call — or instruct someone to do so — unless the skipper is no longer able to give orders.
footer_text: Check regularly that data is still current | Printed on {date} | www.maresafe.eu
info_what_body_location: This card is configured for the Netherlands.
co2_contribution: MareSafe will contribute 1.5% of your purchase to removing CO₂ from the atmosphere.
modal_load_body: Load the .json backup file that was sent to you via email or similar. Your current data will be replaced with the contents of the file.
modal_load_tip: Search your inbox for MareSafe or maresafe to find your backup email.
modal_load_drop: Drop your file here or click to browse
modal_load_error: Invalid file — could not load data.
modal_clear_body: Are you sure you want to clear all form data? This cannot be undone — all vessel and contact information will be permanently removed.
load_invalid_body: The file could not be loaded. Make sure you selected the right backup file and try again.
download_tagline: Choose your languages, pay once — download instantly.
payment_success_body: Payment received — check your email for your download code.
fire: Stop engine — turn off fuel supply
Radio: PAN PAN — or MAYDAY if lives are at risk
Life jackets on for everyone on board
Grab fire extinguisher — do not open door towards the fire
Prepare liferaft / dinghy if situation escalates
mob: Shout "MAN OVERBOARD" — keep eyes on the person and keep pointing
Aim a lifebuoy at the head of the person in the water — also throw a line or jerrycan
MAYDAY on radio — report position and number of persons on board
Return to person — approach downwind, keep engine running, put propeller out of gear near person
Get person on board — warmth, dry clothes, medical check
signals: Distress signal (vessel in distress) — min. 6 blasts
I am turning around via starboard
I am turning around via port
I intend to overtake you on your starboard side
I intend to overtake you on your port side
I am entering harbour via starboard
I am entering harbour via port
